sources: Fierce Biotech Top 10 Pharma R&D Budgets 2024 (https://www.fiercebiotech.com/special-reports/top-10-pharma-rd-budgets-2024)
Top Global CROs by Revenue - PBC Group (https://www.thepbcgroup.com/post/top-global-cros-by-revenue)
Grand View Research Clinical Trial Patient Recruitment Market (https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/clinical-trial-patient-recruitment-services-market-report)

contacts.notes: World's largest pharma R&D spender; #1 in completed trials in 2024. Known as MSD outside the US. Already appears in TrialScreen's LinkedIn network (MSD referenced in posts). Keytruda franchise drives massive oncology trial volume globally.
#2 global pharma by R&D spend. Strong oncology and neuroscience portfolio. Top 5 in completed trials 2024. Genentech is its US arm. Key target for REFER Premium given global trial scale.
Pharma arm (Janssen) runs trials across oncology, immunology, neuroscience. Among the highest trial volumes globally. Multiple clinical operations hubs in US, Belgium, and NJ.

Led in successful trials in 2024. UK-headquartered - particularly relevant for TrialScreen's UK expansion. Named top enrolling pharma sponsor by AstraZeneca itself awarded Velocity as top partner Q1 2025.
Largest pharma by absolute employee count. #2 in completed trials 2024. Acquired Seagen to build major oncology ADC pipeline — adds significant trial volume. High-value REFER Premium target.
Major Swiss pharma with global trial operations across oncology, cardiovascular, and neuroscience. US operational hub in East Hanover, NJ. Top 5 in successful trial completions 2024.
Post-Humira diversification driving immunology, oncology, and neuroscience trial expansion. Highly active in patient recruitment innovation. Largest market cap pharma in 2024.

Explosive growth driven by obesity/diabetes (Mounjaro, Zepbound) and oncology pipeline. Added notable trial volume in 2024 for GLP-1 indications. One of the largest market cap companies globally in 2024.
Top 5 in completed trials 2024. Opdivo/Keytruda competitor driving massive oncology trial volume. High-value target for TrialScreen given immuno-oncology pipeline depth.

UK-headquartered; strong respiratory, oncology, vaccines pipeline. Made notable gains in successful trial completions in 2024. Key target for UK relationship-building given co-location with TrialScreen.
European pharma giant; Dupixent (dupilumab) franchise driving immunology and inflammatory disease trials. US R&D hub in Cambridge, MA. Strong APAC trial activity.
Appears in TrialScreen's LinkedIn network. GLP-1 franchise (Ozempic, Wegovy) and expanding cardiovascular/renal pipeline driving massive recruitment need. Global obesity trial leader.

Top US biotech/pharma. MariTide obesity drug Phase 2 data released 2024. Strong oncology and cardiovascular portfolio. Acquired Horizon Therapeutics (rare disease) — expands trial pipeline significantly.
Major HIV/oncology portfolio. Lenacapavir HIV PrEP (PURPOSE trials) showed 100% efficacy in 2024 — major trial program. Trodelvy ADC driving oncology trial expansion.
German pharma giant with strong cardiovascular, oncology, and women's health trial programs. Significant US R&D presence in Whippany, NJ. Active in global decentralized trials.

Japan's largest pharma, with global operations. Strong rare disease and oncology pipeline. APAC anchor — relevant for TrialScreen's APAC expansion. Significant GI, neuroscience, oncology trial volume.
Privately held German pharma. Strong respiratory, cardiovascular, immunology pipeline. Significant ongoing COPD, HF, and metabolic disease trials. No stock market pressure — long-term R&D commitment.
US biotech leader. Dupixent (co-developed with Sanofi) marketed for 8+ autoimmune conditions. Gene therapy and fianlimab (LAG-3) pipeline expanding trial volume. Strong oncology growth.

Neuroscience-focused biotech. Lecanemab (Leqembi) Alzheimer's approval in 2023 created significant ongoing trial pipeline. ALS, MS, and neurodegeneration are core areas — all have major patient recruitment challenges.

CF franchise leader (Trikafta). Diversifying into pain, kidney disease, sickle cell, and type 1 diabetes via new modalities. Casgevy gene therapy co-developed with CRISPR Therapeutics — rare disease trial expansion.

mRNA platform leader post-COVID. Personalized cancer vaccine (mRNA-4157/V940) in Phase 3 with Merck. RSV, flu/COVID combo, and metabolic disease programs expand trial needs. Digital-forward — achieved 80% minority enrollment in vaccine trials via digital platforms.

RNAi therapeutics pioneer. 5 approved drugs; 25+ programs in clinical development by end 2025. HELIOS-B vutrisiran TTR data was landmark in 2024. Rare disease focus = challenging patient recruitment = high TrialScreen value.

Hematology and oncology specialist. Jakafi (ruxolitinib) blockbuster driving GvHD/myelofibrosis trials. Active pipeline in oncology and inflammatory conditions. Mid-cap biotech with dedicated clinical ops teams.

COVID vaccine pioneer pivoting to oncology mRNA therapeutics. 12+ cancer treatments in trials. ONC-392 (Phase 3) and NT323 (breast cancer) in late-stage. Growing patient recruitment complexity in oncology.
Acquired by Pfizer for $43B in 2023. ADC (antibody-drug conjugate) platform — 4 marketed ADCs. Clinical ops teams may operate semi-independently within Pfizer. Note as Pfizer Oncology contact.
Irish-headquartered pharma. FDA approvals in 2024 (Ziihera for biliary tract cancer). Strong oncology/hematology and CNS/sleep medicine portfolios. Relevant for UK/EU trial operations.
Japanese pharma. Enhertu (trastuzumab deruxtecan) ADC partnership with AstraZeneca — massive global oncology trial program. APAC anchor for TrialScreen. Leading ADC clinical trial volume globally.

Japanese pharma. Leqembi (lecanemab) Alzheimer's drug with Biogen generating ongoing open-label extension studies. Alzheimer's patient recruitment is among the most challenging — direct TrialScreen value proposition.
Belgian specialty pharma focused on neurology and immunology. Bimzelx (bimekizumab) approvals driving IL-17 trials. Strong EU/UK presence. High-value target for TrialScreen's UK market.
Japanese pharma with US headquarters in Northbrook, IL. Strong oncology (Padcev, Xospata) and urology pipelines. APAC-anchor and US mid-tier target for TrialScreen.

World's largest CRO by revenue ($15.4B FY2024). Operates in 100+ countries. Technology & Analytics Solutions a major revenue pillar. Patient recruitment is embedded in their R&D Solutions division. Strategic partnership with IQVIA could unlock massive trial referral volume for TrialScreen.
PPD acquired by Thermo Fisher for $17.4B in 2021. PPD division is a leading full-service CRO. Geographic distribution: US $22.5B, Europe $10.9B, APAC $8B. Key REFER partner opportunity.

Second-largest CRO globally ($8.28B FY2024 revenue). Acquired PRA Health Sciences in 2021. Operates in 53 countries / 93 countries via remote staff. 760+ oncology studies to date. Ireland HQ makes it key for UK and EU engagement. Highly relevant TrialScreen partner.

Covance Drug Development is the CRO arm of Labcorp. Spun off Fortrea in 2023. End-to-end drug development services from early-stage research to regulatory approval. Major patient recruitment outsourcing partner opportunity.

One of the largest CROs globally. Private since 2017 (Pamplona Capital). Strong late-phase and full-service capabilities. Notable GSK, AstraZeneca client relationships. Strategic REFER partner opportunity.

Ashley Moultrie (Syneos Health) co-authored content with Elisa Cascade (TrialScreen) — confirmed warm relationship. Taken private in August 2023 ($7B deal). Biopharmaceutical solutions org with clinical (75% rev) and commercial (25% rev) divisions. Key existing partner to nurture.

Mid-sized CRO growing rapidly (11.8% revenue growth FY2024). Specialized in oncology, rare disease, and metabolic disorders. 44 countries. Strong Roche and Gilead client base. Niche positioning aligns well with TrialScreen rare disease capabilities.

Strong preclinical and early-phase CRO. Listed as top CRO in clinical trials market reports. While primarily preclinical, early-phase clinical operations create patient recruitment needs. Pharmaceutical client list includes all major pharma companies.

Spun out from Labcorp's CRO segment in 2023. Returned to Covance roots. Full-service Phase I-IV clinical services. Divested Endpoint Clinical and Fortrea Patient Access. Growing independently — active CRO procurement cycles in progress.

Asia-Pacific's leading CRO. 3,000 employees; investment from GIC, Temasek, and TPG in 2025. Grown from 300-person (2017) to 3,000-person global organization. APAC expertise is direct complement to TrialScreen's APAC expansion. Highest-priority APAC CRO target.

Mid-sized, flexible full-service CRO with 30+ years experience. Strong Pfizer and J&J client relationships. 4,170 employees. Appeared in Grand View Research patient recruitment market report as key player. Good mid-tier CRO partner target.

UK-headquartered specialty CRO. Focused on rare disease and oncology subsets. PrimeVigilance (pharmacovigilance arm) adds breadth. UK HQ aligns perfectly with TrialScreen's UK market. Growing 22%+ annually.
UK-headquartered statistics and data management CRO. Specializes in biometrics/biostatistics — complementary to recruitment needs. UK location makes it a natural UK-market touchpoint for TrialScreen.

Global leader in IRB services and clinical research solutions. Published 2024 Report on Top Issues Impacting Clinical Research Sites. IRB + site support services position WCG at the intersection of sites and sponsors — strong TrialScreen partnership opportunity.

APAC-focused CRO with academic roots (The George Institute for Global Health). Strong cardiovascular, stroke, and chronic disease expertise. Novartis and GSK client base. Key APAC CRO target for TrialScreen.

One of the largest US site networks. 70+ sites across US and Europe. Recognized by AstraZeneca as top enrolling site network Q1 2025. FibroScan, PBMC, in-house lab capabilities. Supported 9 FDA-approved products in 2024. Direct integration opportunity for TrialScreen patient referral.

US site network including Artemis Institute sites. Focus on diverse patient populations. Growing site network actively seeking new site partners. Embedded and dedicated clinical research sites across California and other states.
Florida-based site network with dedicated clinical research sites. Active across multiple therapeutic areas. Florida patient population offers strong demographic diversity for TrialScreen referrals.

contacts.sites: Multiple sites in Texas, California, and Nevada
contacts.notes: Fully integrated, wholly owned site network. 280,000+ prescreened participants in proprietary database. Hospital-affiliated sites. Centralized management and SOPs. Diverse participant populations — strong alignment with TrialScreen's diversity mission.

contacts.sites: Multiple sites in Texas and Louisiana
contacts.notes: Texas-based dedicated clinical research organization. Multi-therapeutic area expertise. Active Phase 1-4 trial sites. Relevant for TrialScreen US market penetration in the South/Southwest.

San Antonio-based clinical research network. Active in Phase 1-4 trials across diverse therapeutic areas. Strong Hispanic patient population access — highly relevant for TrialScreen's diversity and inclusion focus.

contacts.sites: 28+ NIHR Clinical Research Facilities across England
contacts.notes: UK's national clinical research infrastructure. 28 NIHR CRFs awarded £161M (2022-2027). Covers all major NHS hospitals. Critical gateway to UK NHS research sites for TrialScreen's UK expansion. Research Delivery Network (RDN) operates across England.

Appears as 'similar company' on TrialScreen's LinkedIn page — confirmed industry awareness. Only global trade org representing clinical research sites. Global Impact Partner program allows TrialScreen vendor positioning. Members include sites, site networks, and hospitals globally. Critical partnership for site channel distribution.

contacts.sites: Multiple sites in South East Melbourne
contacts.notes: Already connected to TrialScreen (noted in task). Australia's largest health service; 7,000+ beds, catchment of 3.5M people (15% of Australia). 1 in 5 Australian clinical trials conducted at Alfred precinct (Monash). APAC trial delivery hub — key for TrialScreen APAC expansion.

contacts.company: King's Health Partners Clinical Trials Office
contacts.sites: KCL, Guy's & St Thomas', King's College Hospital, South London & Maudsley NHS
contacts.notes: UKCRC-accredited unit; sponsors clinical trials across 4 major London hospitals. Commercial sponsor gateway for pharma access to London NHS sites. Streamlined setup and financial management. Strategic UK site partnership for TrialScreen.

Top specialty cancer hospital in US. Intensive consent flows, imaging endpoints, high oncology trial volume. Over 500 active trials at any time. Key target for TrialScreen oncology patient recruitment solution.
Top US academic medical center. First-in-human trials; neurology, oncology, and infectious disease. Tight safety narratives and consent precision required. Major trial volume across all phases.

Multi-specialty trial leader with sites in MN, AZ, and FL. High protocol diversity across oncology, cardiovascular, and rare disease. Among the largest academic trial programs in the US. Critical target for TrialScreen.
Top cardiovascular and neurological research center globally. Complex device/pharma trial mix. Strong imaging and monitoring capabilities. International reach (Abu Dhabi, London).

contacts.company: Massachusetts General Hospital / Mass General Brigham
contacts.notes: Harvard-affiliated; one of the most active trial networks in the US. Academic and oncology focus. Heavy monitoring cadence. Most recently activated Precision BioSciences ELIMINATE-B Phase 1 (2025). Key TrialScreen target.

Top cardiovascular and pulmonary clinical research center. Close CRA partnership culture. Active clinical trial presence in oncology and cardiovascular. Duke Clinical Research Institute (DCRI) is one of the world's largest academic CROs.

Rated #1 cancer center for 10 consecutive years (US News). High-stakes oncology SAE narratives. 1,300+ active trials. Cell and gene therapy — complex patient recruitment. Top TrialScreen target for oncology segment.
Top US academic medical center. Oncology and transplant focus. Diverse LA patient population — strong diversity trial recruitment fit. Chain-of-custody and complex imaging capabilities.
Top genomics and device clinical research center. Device endpoints and precision medicine emphasis. Strong tech-adjacent culture — receptive to digital recruitment solutions like TrialScreen.
Leading immunology and neuroscience research center. Strong translational research ties. Active across oncology, immunology, and CNS. Benioff Children's Hospital arm adds pediatric trial volume.

UK's largest university by research activity. UCL Clinical Trials Unit (MRC CTU at UCL) is one of the UK's leading academic CTUs. NIHR-funded. Cancer, HIV, and infectious disease focus. Prime UK academic target for TrialScreen.

contacts.active_trials: 1,000+ (of which ~150 interventional in EU; 350+ outside EU)
contacts.notes: Oxford sponsors ~1,000 academic clinical research studies; 5 UKCRC-registered Clinical Trials Units. Centre for Tropical Medicine adds global trial reach. RECOVERY trial (COVID) demonstrated Oxford's high-volume trial capacity. Strategic UK academic target.

UKCRC-accredited King's Clinical Trials Unit (KCTU). Part of King's Health Partners consortium. Primarily NIHR-funded multicentre trials. Commercial team supports pharma sponsor access. Relevant for TrialScreen UK market.

Cambridge Clinical Trials Unit (CCTU) — science-led NIHR UKCRC registered academic CTU. Broad spectrum of interventional trials. Cambridge University Hospitals NHS Trust co-location. UK academic target for TrialScreen.

NIHR Imperial Clinical Research Facility (NIHR Imperial CRF). Strong cardiovascular, infectious disease, and immunology programs. Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust partner. UK AMIIC facility used in FORTIFY international trial. Key London academic target.

Australia's top-ranked research university. Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre partnership for oncology. Australian Clinical Trials Alliance (ACTA) member. Strong global cancer trial consortia involvement. Key APAC academic target for TrialScreen.

contacts.active_trials: 300+ (accounts for ~25% of all Australian clinical trial activity)
contacts.notes: Connected to TrialScreen via Monash Health relationship. Top 50 global university; Australia's largest. Monash-led trials account for ~25% of Australian clinical trial activity. 5 affiliated health services, 7,000+ hospital beds, 3,000 GP network. Monash-ClinChoice partnership announced 2026. Highest-priority APAC academic target.

Major Australian research university. NHMRC Clinical Trials Centre (CTC) is one of Australia's leading academic CTUs — cardiovascular, oncology, and respiratory focus. George Institute partner. Key APAC academic target.

Singapore's leading research institution. Duke-NUS Medical School provides translational medicine backbone. Core partner in Asia-specific vaccine trials (dengue, COVID). Partners with Pfizer, Novartis, GSK under global protocols. Singapore regulatory speed (6-8 weeks) is key APAC advantage.

Leading US rare disease patient advocacy umbrella organization. 900+ attendees at 2024 NORD Breakthrough Summit. 30+ advocacy organizations in rare cancer coalition. Advocating for greater decentralization and digital health in clinical trials. Direct alignment with TrialScreen's rare disease capabilities.

Largest Alzheimer's PAG. Manages TrialMatch platform (competitive intelligence). Alzheimer's trial recruitment is among the hardest — high screen failure rates. Leqembi approval creating massive demand for prevention trial participants. Key TrialScreen value proposition alignment.

Largest cancer PAG globally by revenue. ACS CAN (advocacy arm) and clinical trial awareness campaigns. Strong patient network for oncology recruitment. ACS and ASCO partnership on trial diversity. Partnership opportunity for patient awareness campaigns.

Type 1 diabetes advocacy. Active in clinical trial partnerships for immune therapies. Teplizumab (first T1D disease-modifying drug, 2022) was driven partly through JDRF's T1D Exchange registry. Registry-based recruitment model is complementary to TrialScreen.

CFF is a leading model for PAG-driven drug development — funded Vertex's Trikafta via venture philanthropy. Maintains CF patient registry and trial network. High potential TrialScreen partner for CF-adjacent rare disease trials.

National lupus advocacy organization. Lupus clinical trials have significant patient recruitment challenges — autoimmune disease, primarily affecting women of color. Diversity in recruitment is a critical goal. TrialScreen's targeted platform has direct value here.

LUNGevity Foundation (merged with Lung Cancer Alliance) is the largest lung cancer PAG in the US. Active in clinical trial awareness. Highly active oncology trial landscape — KRAS, EGFR, PD-1 trials all require specialist patient recruitment.

Largest US mental health PAG. CNS and psychiatry trials face unique recruitment challenges — stigma, complex eligibility. Growing pipeline in depression, schizophrenia (KarXT approved 2024), and bipolar disorder. TrialScreen CNS capabilities relevant here.

Already connected to TrialScreen — provided a testimonial (noted in task). APAC patient advocacy group for systemic sclerosis (scleroderma). Rare autoimmune disease with significant unmet trial recruitment need. Pilot relationship for APAC PAG engagement strategy.

Global MS PAG federation. MS trials are a major CRO and pharma focus area (BTK inhibitors, anti-CD20 agents). Significant UK membership base — aligned with TrialScreen UK market expansion. Global reach across APAC and US chapters too.

Already connected to TrialScreen (noted in task). Only non-profit solely dedicated to clinical research professionals. 20,000+ members globally. ACRP Annual Conference is a top industry event. Conference sponsorship, speaking, and exhibit opportunities provide direct access to clinical ops decision-makers.

Global member organization for life sciences professionals. DIA 2024 Global Annual Meeting (60th anniversary) was held June 16-20. Brings together industry, regulators, academics, and patients. DIA membership provides access to regulatory and clinical development decision-makers across all major pharma companies.

contacts.company: SCOPE Summit (Summit for Clinical Ops Executives)
contacts.notes: SCOPE Summit 2024: 4,000 participants, 275 exhibits, 1,200 organizations, 29 countries. 58% of participants hold decision-making roles. Premier event for clinical operations executives — VP Clinical Operations, Head of Patient Recruitment, Clinical Innovation. Exhibit, sponsorship, or speaking slot would directly reach TrialScreen's primary buyer personas.

World's largest biotech trade association. BIO International Convention 2024: 19,500+ attendees, 70+ countries, 61,000+ partnering meetings. Primarily biotech executives and investors — pipeline companies are early-stage clinical trial buyers. Brand visibility and B2B partnering opportunities for TrialScreen.

Conference series bringing together patient advocates, pharma, CROs, and regulators. Focus on patient-centered trial design and recruitment. Strong alignment with TrialScreen's patient-first positioning. Speaking opportunities would position TrialScreen as thought leader in patient engagement.

Non-profit consortium of major pharma companies (including most TrialScreen Segment 1 targets). Drives industry-wide clinical trial innovation including centralized IRB models, diversity initiatives, and trial simplification. Membership includes all top-tier pharma. Engagement here could create warm introductions to multiple pharma sponsor contacts simultaneously.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Digital patient recruitment platform. Partnered with SEQSTER (2024) to use EHR/genomic data for patient matching. Precision recruitment focus. Listed in Grand View Research as key market player. Direct TrialScreen competitor in digital patient recruitment.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Acquired by M&B Sciences in March 2022. Technology-driven CRO focused on patient-centric trial access. 24/7 live patient navigators. Small company post-acquisition; unclear current trajectory. Listed as market competitor.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. AI platform for clinical trial patient recruitment. Claims 33% reduction in recruitment timelines. Modern Clinical Trial Marketplace concept — 3-sided platform (patients, sites, sponsors). Growing AI-based matching capability. Direct TrialScreen competitor.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Rebranded from TrialSpark in Dec 2023. Evolved from clinical trial optimizer to full-stack drug developer. Partnership with 23andMe for trial recruitment. Completed recruitment 2.5x faster than industry standard. Unique hybrid model — not pure recruitment play but competes in the space.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Full-service patient recruitment and retention technology company. 6M+ patients reached; 3.1M+ patients connected to studies. Social media and indication-specific expertise. Listed as key market player in Grand View Research report.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. European patient recruitment platform; ClinLife technology. Online patient recruitment, retention, surveys, and site selection. Strong EU presence with growing US operations. Listed as key market player alongside Antidote and Clara in multiple market reports.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Direct-to-patient digital advertising and engagement for clinical trial recruitment. AI-powered EMR Capture & Intelligence completed April 2024. 96 employees, ~$16.8M revenue. Proprietary digital marketing platform. Listed in Grand View Research market report.

COMPETITOR — monitor, do not pitch. Patient recruitment marketplace connecting pre-screened, opt-in patients to clinical trials. Focus on rare and chronic diseases. Listed by market research sources as a key clinical trial recruitment player.

Targeted protein degradation (PROTAC) platform. Lead drug vepdegestrant in Phase 3 breast cancer trials (two Ph3 underway). Novel modality — patient recruitment challenges for biomarker-selected populations.
Precision medicine company (kinase inhibitors). Ayvakit (avapritinib) for GIST and SM. Rare disease patient populations with significant recruitment challenges.
Base editing pioneer. BEACON trial in sickle cell. Rare disease/gene therapy patient populations are extremely challenging to recruit. High value for TrialScreen rare disease targeting.
TIL (tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte) cell therapy company. Amtagvi (lifileucel) FDA-approved for melanoma 2024. Advanced oncology trials in cervical, lung, and other solid tumors.

Subsidiary of Sumitovant (Sumitomo). Relugolix franchise (Myfembree for endometriosis/uterine fibroids; Orgovyx for prostate cancer). Women's health and oncology trials with complex patient recruitment.
